prose:
Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = 7.4357165 eV

Method–provenance block

• Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) gap of ethylene, in electron-volts (eV).
• Level of theory: Density-functional theory, tier “standard” → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (PySCF backend).
• Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz; no further optimisation (notation: DFT//user-provided geometry).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software: chemkit frontier-orbitals skill → PySCF 2.x.
• Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 6 cycles; integrity.trustworthy = true. No warnings reported by the engine.

Key numerical outputs (gas-phase B3LYP/def2-TZVP):
• Total electronic energy: –78.623486 Eh (–2139.4541 eV)
• HOMO: –7.6155818 eV
• LUMO: –0.17986527 eV
• HOMO–LUMO gap: 7.4357165 eV ← headline value

Reproducibility: live engine log was written to the frontier-orbitals .out file announced at launch; result JSON saved at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpe5sjd318/live.json. The CLI invocation recorded in that JSON reproduces the run exactly.

No warnings were reported by the tool.
